methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Deep Isolation, a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Elizabeth Muller, is evaluated against a peer set of 3 public and 8 private nuclear technology firms, including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. The company has raised $26M to date.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable here, using the $26M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to next-stage. This approach yields $52M. Peer-median triangulation is not applicable; while 8 same-sector comparables exist, only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum needed for reliable median derivation. A broad all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $44M to $60M, with a median point of $52M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty given the sparse comparable universe in nuclear energy's early-stage venture market. Key risks to valuation include technology validation timelines, regulatory pathway clarity for the underlying nuclear approach, and the maturity disparity between Deep Isolation and later-stage peers like TerraPower and X-Energy. The narrow methodological foundation warrants follow-on valuation review as additional comparable transactions or financings emerge in this sector.
